items.title: Evaluate on held-out labels
items.avoid: Building confusion matrices from training-set predictions or from data used during feature selection without a fresh holdout.
items.prefer: Compute TP/FP/TN/FN on validation for threshold tuning; report test matrix once after final model and cutoff are frozen.
items.why: Optimistic counts mis-set stakeholder expectations for false alarms and misses at launch.
items.example: Fit on train; y_pred = (predict_proba(X_val)[:,1] >= t).astype(int); confusion_matrix(y_val, y_pred).

items.title: Declare layout and positive class
items.avoid: Publishing a 2×2 table without stating rows/columns and which label is positive.
items.prefer: Caption: 'Rows=actual, cols=predicted, positive=spam(1)' and show TN, FP, FN, TP explicitly.
items.why: Readers cannot verify TPR/FPR or compare to other reports without convention.
items.example: Report: TP=120, FP=15, TN=800, FN=65 (positive=fraud, sklearn layout).

items.title: Report minority-class errors explicitly
items.avoid: Stopping at accuracy when positives are rare.
items.prefer: Show the full matrix plus TPR (TP/(TP+FN)) and FPR (FP/(FP+TN)) for the positive class.
items.why: Business usually cares about misses (FN) or false alarms (FP) on the minority event — accuracy hides them.
items.example: Fraud: 'Accuracy 99.1%; TPR 72% (28% of fraud missed); FPR 0.4%'.

items.title: Pick threshold before final matrix
items.avoid: Using default 0.5 when deployment needs a different operating point.
items.prefer: Choose threshold from validation ROC/PR or cost analysis, then build the matrix at that t.
items.why: The matrix describes production behavior only at the cutoff you will ship.
items.example: Select t where TPR≥0.90 from roc_curve, then confusion_matrix at that t — not at 0.5 by habit.
